req.title: Bulk data export
req.statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-016 mandates that a provisioned bulk client can trigger a FHIR $export operation, receive a 202 with a Content-Location poll URL, and ultimately obtain NDJSON output for large datasets paginated across multiple files. Three orthogonal test scenarios are designed: (1) EP positive – an authorised client gets a 202 and the job eventually yields NDJSON; (2) NEG negative – an unauthorised client is rejected with 403; (3) BVA boundary – the last page of a paginated NDJSON export contains exactly zero additional resources (empty sentinel), verifying the boundary between the final page and end-of-stream. Each test uses a shared fixture for the base URL and auth tokens, and delegates polling/parsing to concise helper placeholders to stay within the 40-line limit.

tests.name: test_authorised_bulk_export_queued_and_produces_ndjson
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client is provisioned with a valid bulk-export OAuth2 token (scope: system/*.read)
At least one FHIR resource exists in the server
actions: Send GET /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async using the authorised token
Assert HTTP 202 is returned with a Content-Location header
Poll the Content-Location URL until status 200 (job complete) or timeout
Retrieve the first output URL from the manifest
expected: Content-Location header is present and is a valid URL
Completed manifest contains an 'output' array with at least one entry
Each output entry has type 'ndjson' and a valid url field
NDJSON file contains at least one valid JSON object per line
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time

def test_authorised_bulk_export_queued_and_produces_ndjson(
    bulk_auth_headers, fhir_base_url, poll_until_complete
):
    resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={
            **bulk_auth_headers,
            "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
            "Prefer": "respond-async",
        },
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}"
    content_location = resp.headers.get("Content-Location")
    assert content_location, "Content-Location header missing"

    manifest = poll_until_complete(content_location, bulk_auth_headers)
    assert "output" in manifest, "Manifest missing 'output' key"
    assert len(manifest["output"]) >= 1, "No output files in manifest"

    first_url = manifest["output"][0]["url"]
    ndjson_resp = <http_client>.get(first_url, headers=bulk_auth_headers)
    assert ndjson_resp.status_code == 200
    lines = [l for l in ndjson_resp.text.splitlines() if l.strip()]
    assert len(lines) >= 1, "NDJSON file is empty"
    import json
    for line in lines:
        obj = json.loads(line)
        assert "resourceType" in obj

tests.name: test_unauthorised_client_receives_403_on_bulk_export
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A token exists that does NOT have the bulk-export scope (or is entirely absent)
actions: Send GET /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async using an invalid/missing token
Inspect the HTTP response status code and body
expected: Response body is a FHIR OperationOutcome with severity 'error'
tests.executable:
import json
import pytest

def test_unauthorised_client_receives_403_on_bulk_export(
    no_auth_headers, fhir_base_url
):
    resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={
            **no_auth_headers,
            "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
            "Prefer": "respond-async",
        },
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert "Content-Location" not in resp.headers, \
        "Content-Location must not be present for rejected request"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", \
        "Body must be an OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues), \
        "OperationOutcome must contain at least one error-severity issue"

tests.name: test_paginated_export_last_page_is_empty_boundary
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client is provisioned with a valid bulk-export token
Server is seeded with exactly N resources where N equals the page-size limit (so page 1 is full and page 2 is empty/absent)
actions: Send GET /$export with _count equal to the server page size using the authorised token
Poll Content-Location until manifest is ready
Collect all output file URLs from the manifest
Fetch every output file and count total NDJSON lines
Attempt to fetch a hypothetical next-page URL (page index = len(output) + 1) to verify no additional page exists
expected: Manifest 'output' array length equals the expected number of pages for exact page-size dataset
Total NDJSON lines across all pages equals exactly the seeded resource count
Requesting a page beyond the last returns 404 or is absent from the manifest
No line on the final page is blank or malformed
trace: R-HLT-016: large datasets shall be paginated
BVA – boundary between last populated page and end-of-stream
tests.executable:
import json
import pytest

PAGE_SIZE = <get_server_page_size>()
SEEDED_COUNT = PAGE_SIZE

def test_paginated_export_last_page_is_empty_boundary(
    bulk_auth_headers, fhir_base_url, poll_until_complete,
    seed_resources, cleanup_resources
):
    seed_resources(count=SEEDED_COUNT, resource_type="Patient")
    try:
        resp = <http_client>.get(
            f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
            params={"_type": "Patient", "_count": PAGE_SIZE},
            headers={
                **bulk_auth_headers,
                "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
                "Prefer": "respond-async",
            },
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 202
        manifest = poll_until_complete(
            resp.headers["Content-Location"], bulk_auth_headers
        )
        output_files = manifest.get("output", [])
        total_lines = 0
        for entry in output_files:
            ndjson = <http_client>.get(entry["url"], headers=bulk_auth_headers)
            assert ndjson.status_code == 200
            lines = [l for l in ndjson.text.splitlines() if l.strip()]
            for line in lines:
                json.loads(line)
            total_lines += len(lines)
        assert total_lines == SEEDED_COUNT, \
            f"Expected {SEEDED_COUNT} lines, got {total_lines}"
        assert "next" not in manifest, "No next-page link expected at boundary"
    finally:
        cleanup_resources(resource_type="Patient")
